functional.description: L1 framework core (.aiox-core/core/) must be protected from writes
functional.rationale: Framework core is immutable, deny rules in .claude/settings.json enforce this
acceptance: Deny rules block all Write/Edit to L1

functional.description: L2 framework templates must be extend-only (no overwrites)
functional.rationale: Templates are read-only to prevent accidental framework corruption

functional.description: L4 project runtime (docs/stories/, squads/, tests/) must be always mutable
functional.rationale: Project code must be writable for development velocity

functional.description: Layer boundaries must be enforced at hook layer (PreToolUse)
functional.rationale: Deterministic enforcement of framework boundaries
acceptance: enforce-quality-gates.cjs detects and blocks cross-layer violations

functional.description: @aiox-master must route all tasks to appropriate specialized agents
functional.rationale: Orchestrator pattern ensures single point of task routing

functional.description: @devops authority (Art. II) must be enforced exclusively for git push/PR
functional.rationale: Non-negotiable constitutional enforcement: only @devops can push to remote

functional.description: Agent authority must be explicit in .claude/rules/agent-authority.md
functional.rationale: Canonical source of truth for who can do what
acceptance: Agent matrix exists and is up-to-date

functional.description: Agent registry (entity-registry.yaml) must map task types → responsible agents
functional.rationale: Registry lookups enable deterministic task routing

functional.description: PRDs must be structured (FRs/NFRs/edge cases/gates, 30-50 lines)
functional.rationale: Spec-driven determinism: precise requirements enable reproducible testing

functional.description: Stories must have precise AC (Given/When/Then format, 5-10 ACs)
functional.rationale: Measurable acceptance criteria enable deterministic testing
acceptance: All story AC are in Given/When/Then format

functional.description: Acceptance criteria must be 100% traceable to requirements
functional.rationale: No invented criteria: everything traces back to FR/NFR
acceptance: Gap traceability matrix created in story 12.6

functional.description: CodeRabbit auto-review must detect pattern violations
functional.rationale: Automated quality validation catches out-of-spec code

functional.description: Agent activation must load Tier A (Constitution + rules) deterministically
functional.rationale: 95%+ context coverage requires deterministic loading of core context

functional.description: Shim must auto-load persona (102 ln shim → 937+ ln context)
functional.rationale: Context expansion: shim activation reads persona + tasks + workflows + memory

functional.description: Context loading must complete in <500ms on agent activation
functional.rationale: Performance SLO: agent responsiveness critical for UX

functional.description: Agent memory must persist between sessions (handoff protocol)
functional.rationale: Session continuity: memory carries forward across contexts

functional.description: 7 Constitutional gates must be active and enforced
functional.rationale: Non-negotiable: all 7 articles (I-VII) must be enforced

functional.description: Gate decisions must be audit-logged (JSONL format)
functional.rationale: Auditability: every gate decision recorded for compliance

functional.description: No gate can fail silently
functional.rationale: Transparency: all gate verdicts recorded and metricated

functional.description: Art. IV (No Invention) must prevent spec statements without traceability
functional.rationale: Constitutional enforcement: no invented features

nonFunctional.metric: 31/31 gaps traced to story or escalation
100% (memory carries forward, zero loss between sessions)

constraints.impact: Unfixable L1/L2 gaps must be escalated via *propose-modification, not fixed in stories
Large context files must be cached or lazy-loaded

constraints.description: Gate logging must not block agent execution
constraints.impact: Gate-logger must be async or circuit-breaker protected

constraints.description: 100% agent coverage required for Phase 1 completion
constraints.impact: All 12 agents (12.1-12.12) must pass QA gate

constraints.description: 31 operational gaps must be explained or escalated
constraints.impact: Story 12.6 must create traceability matrix with decision per gap

constraints.description: No secrets or credentials in framework code
constraints.impact: All API keys, tokens must be externalized to environment/vault

constraints.description: Audit trail required for all gate decisions
constraints.impact: Every gate verdict must be logged to .aiox/gate-logs/

assumptions.description: Agent files (.claude/agents/*, .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md) exist and are valid
Morgan's audit (31 gaps + 21 ambiguities) is complete and accurate
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Testing may miss critical gaps, false sense of completion

assumptions.description: Constitution (.aiox-core/constitution.md) is the source of truth for Art. I-VII
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Gate implementations may diverge from canonical constitution

assumptions.description: Agent memory files persist and are accessible between sessions
CodeRabbit integration is working and auto-healing loops function
assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Self-healing fails, manual QA intervention required

domainModel.description: AI persona with specific authority and responsibilities
Development unit with acceptance criteria and testing tasks
Hierarchical boundary: L1 (core), L2 (templates), L3 (config), L4 (runtime)
Identified operational or infrastructure gap from Morgan's audit

states.error: Agent activation failed → show error reason + remediation
states.empty: No context loaded → fallback to minimal agent definition

interactions.trigger: Story status transition (Draft → Ready → InProgress → InReview → Done)

states.error: Status transition blocked → story remains in current state
states.empty: Story has no status field → default to Draft
No gate config found → allow (fail-safe for new gates)

states.error: Memory file corrupted → start with clean slate + warn
states.empty: No memory file → first session, no prior context

edgeCases.scenario: Agent activation fails due to missing persona file
edgeCases.handling: Fallback to minimal agent definition (102 ln shim only); display error + remediation path
Override available with --skip-{gate} flag + audit-log the override; escalate to @aiox-master

edgeCases.scenario: Story status field missing or malformed
edgeCases.handling: Validate on load; if missing, set to Draft; if malformed, normalize to nearest valid state

edgeCases.scenario: Agent memory file not persisted between sessions
edgeCases.handling: Handoff artifact available in .aiox/handoffs/; re-load from YAML if memory.md missing

edgeCases.scenario: Circular dependency between agents (A needs B needs A)
edgeCases.handling: Detect at registry load time; log error; fail-fast with clear message

edgeCases.scenario: CodeRabbit auto-healing loop exceeds max iterations (e.g., 2)
edgeCases.handling: Halt auto-healing; log issue; require manual review; do not proceed to QA

terminology.definition: AI persona with specific expertise, commands, and authority scope; interchangeable executor within its domain
Development unit with acceptance criteria, estimated effort, and testing tasks; atomic contract
Enforcement mechanism for constitutional articles (Art. I-VII); blocks or allows operations based on rules
Identified operational or infrastructure shortcoming from audit; remediated or escalated
Exclusive responsibility for specific operations (e.g., @devops exclusive for git push); non-delegable
Framework hierarchy: L1 (core, immutable), L2 (templates, extend-only), L3 (config, mutable), L4 (runtime, always mutable)
Precise, measurable conditions in Given/When/Then format; story is Done when ALL AC pass
Transfer of context between agents or sessions; memory + decision log preserved in YAML

openQuestions.question: Are all 31 gaps from Morgan's audit still valid, or have some been resolved since Cont 42?
Can gate-logging be async without risking out-of-order writes to JSONL?
What is the Performance SLO for CodeRabbit auto-healing? Should it have timeout?
Should agent memory be versioned (v1, v2) to support backwards compatibility?
How should gate overrides be tracked and approved? Self-service or approval chain?
